description: You are a historical fiction author researching the lighting conditions during the first Jack the Ripper murder in Whitechapel, London. Reconstruct the sky as it appeared on August 31, 1888, at 03:00 UTC from London, UK (51.5° N, 0.1° W). Configure the view to include the landscape, atmosphere, cardinal points, and azimuthal grid. Capture a screenshot centered on the Moon that clearly shows its phase and position relative to the horizon, saved to ~/Pictures/stellarium/. Record the date, time, target, a description of the Moon's phase, and its cardinal direction in a text file at ~/Desktop/whitechapel_moon.txt. Ensure all program settings, including the date and time, are saved.
